raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A broad request to check the excerpt centrally includes whether the technical and argumentative chain from architecture to abstention rule to generality is internally coherent.","R1.3":"The artifact makes quantitative and qualitative claims based on reported evaluation results, so checking whether those claims are supported is central.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is minimal and the artifact is prose from a paper draft, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and register are centrally expected.","RX.1":"Any projection of a vague checking request must preserve uncertainty about the exact intended review depth.","RX.2":"The excerpt contains strong claims such as first-system, any-instruction, and arbitrary-environment claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.","RX.3":"The response must stay within responsibility projection rather than performing the requested check or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns robot execution versus abstention under ambiguity, downstream-harm considerations remain active for claims about safe behavior.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact, especially the broad wording and the stated experimental claims."}}

rationale.R1.2: A broad request to check the excerpt centrally includes whether the technical and argumentative chain from architecture to abstention rule to generality is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes quantitative and qualitative claims based on reported evaluation results, so checking whether those claims are supported is central.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is minimal and the artifact is prose from a paper draft, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and register are centrally expected.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague checking request must preserve uncertainty about the exact intended review depth.
rationale.RX.2: The excerpt contains strong claims such as first-system, any-instruction, and arbitrary-environment claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within responsibility projection rather than performing the requested check or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns robot execution versus abstention under ambiguity, downstream-harm considerations remain active for claims about safe behavior.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact, especially the broad wording and the stated experimental claims.
